source_jobs.title: Fractional HRBP / HR Generalist
source_jobs.description:
Fractional HRBP / HR Generalist

M&A Experience Required | Paylocity Required | Hybrid | Chicago, IL

Contract (3 months, option to convert) | $50-$75/hr

TALENT COMMUNITY NOTICE

This position is part of livingHR's talent community. By applying, you are expressing interest in joining our network of trusted professionals who partner with us on a contract basis when client project needs arise. This is not an immediate or guaranteed opening. Engagements are project-based, and livingHR will reach out when an opportunity aligns with your experience, availability, and location.



POSITION DETAILS

Location: Chicago, IL

Type: Contract / Fractional

Work Model: Hybrid

Compensation: $50 - $75/hr

Duration: 3-month contract with option to convert to permanent hire



WHO WE ARE

livingHR is The Work Agency™, a national boutique HR advisory and consulting firm built to make work better. We partner with organizations to deliver human-centric, future-of-work-ready HR managed services, professional services, and products. We believe in taking exceptional care of our people so they can deliver exceptional outcomes for clients, partners, and communities.



POSITION OVERVIEW

We are seeking an experienced HR Business Partner / Generalist with demonstrated M&A integration expertise to support a client engagement based in Chicago. This hybrid role requires a consultative professional who can operate independently, navigate complex organizational change, and deliver credible HR partnership at pace.



RESPONSIBILITIES

• Deliver human-centric HR solutions that improve workplace experiences

• Support M&A integration activities including org design, cultural onboarding, workforce transitions, and employee communications

• Serve as a trusted advisor throughout the engagement lifecycle

• Apply consulting, project management, and communication best practices

• Partner with livingHR Principals and client stakeholders to drive measurable value

• Manage and optimize HR workflows and employee data within Paylocity

• Track billable work and maintain accurate project documentation

• Align people strategies with broader business objectives



REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

• Paylocity experience is required -- hands-on configuration, reporting, and day-to-day HR operations

• Demonstrated M&A HR experience including due diligence support, integration planning, and workforce transition

• 5+ years in an HRBP or HR Generalist role, ideally within a consulting, professional services, or multi-client environment

• Availability to work on-site in Chicago on a hybrid schedule as client needs require

• Experience working directly with clients in consulting, professional services, PEOs, or agencies

• Strong advisory, stakeholder management, and executive communication skills

• Ability to operate independently in a fast-paced, client-facing environment

• Comfort working in flexible, project-based engagements



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

• PHR, SPHR, SHRM-CP, SHRM-SCP, or platform certifications

• Experience supporting PE-backed or high-growth organizations through rapid change

• Familiarity with multi-state HR compliance, particularly Illinois employment law

• Willingness to travel occasionally as client needs require



WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT

livingHR engages contract consultants on a project-by-project basis. Engagement timing, scope, and duration vary based on client needs. Consultants may be considered for multiple opportunities over time as part of our long-term talent community. This engagement is structured as a 3-month contract with the potential for conversion to a permanent hire based on mutual fit.



livingHR · The Work Agency™ · livinghr.com http://livinghr.com

source_jobs.title: Trust Officer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ORGANIZATION

The mission is to help individuals and families preserve and grow their wealth with integrity, transparency, and a deeply personal touch. The organization serves clients across Southwest Florida and beyond, offering investment management, trust, and estate services with a boutique feel and institutional strength. Their team is close-knit, community-minded, and committed to doing right by their clients and each other.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

As a Trust Officer, you will serve as a trusted advisor to high‑net‑worth clients, overseeing the administration of complex trust accounts and ensuring alignment with fiduciary standards, regulatory requirements, and each client’s long‑term financial objectives. You’ll build deep, meaningful relationships while guiding clients, beneficiaries, and internal partners through the intricacies of trust management with clarity, professionalism, and care.



Day-to-Day Responsibilities

 - Administer a portfolio of trusts, estates, and agency accounts in accordance with governing documents, state and federal regulations, and company policies.

 - Serve as the primary relationship manager for clients and beneficiaries, providing guidance on trust terms, distributions, tax considerations, and estate planning matters.

 - Review and interpret trust instruments, wills, and related legal documents to determine appropriate administrative actions.

 - Coordinate with investment, tax, legal, and compliance partners to ensure cohesive, high‑quality service across all accounts.

 - Monitor account activities, investment performance, cash flow needs, and distribution schedules to ensure accuracy and proactive oversight.

 - Identify risks or compliance issues and address them promptly, escalating concerns as needed to protect client and company interests.

 - Cultivate new business opportunities through exceptional service, referrals, and meaningful participation in client and community engagement efforts.
   
   

What We Are Looking For

 - Bachelor’s degree in finance, business, accounting, or a related field.

 - 7+ years of trust administration, estate planning, or fiduciary services experience, ideally within a bank, trust company, or wealth management environment.

 - Strong understanding of fiduciary law, trust structures, estate settlement processes, tax considerations, and investment concepts.

 - Demonstrated ability to manage complex client relationships with discretion, clarity, and empathy.

 - Excellent analytical, organizational, and document‑interpretation skills with high attention to detail.

 - Proven ability to partner effectively with legal, investment, tax, and compliance teams.
   
   

Nice-to-Have Requirements

 - CTFA designation or progress toward certification.

 - Experience working with Florida-specific trust laws and regulations.

 - Familiarity with trust accounting systems and wealth management software.
   
   

WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE

Working here feels like being part of something meaningful. You’ll be surrounded by smart, kind, and driven colleagues who care deeply about their work and the people they serve.

 - Health & Wellness: 90% employer-paid medical coverage. Dental and vision plans available at employee cost.

 - Financial Benefits: Employer-supported 401(k) safe harbor contribution and Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP).

 - Work-Life Balance: Generous PTO, paid holidays, hybrid policy, and a supportive team culture.

 - Wellbeing: Access to mental health resources and wellness initiatives.

 - Culture: We host annual team events, community involvement opportunities, and foster a respectful, inclusive workplace.



Reports to: Trust Officer

Department: Fiduciary Services

Employment Type: Full-time / Onsite (Remote 2x/month)

Location: Naples, FL OR Fort Myers, FL

source_jobs.title: Compensation Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Compensation Analyst

Market Pricing & Benchmarking | Remote | Contract

Contract (Duration TBD) | $50-$75/hr

TALENT COMMUNITY NOTICE

This position is part of livingHR’s talent community. By applying, you are expressing interest in joining our network of trusted professionals who partner with us on a contract basis when client project needs arise. This is not an immediate or guaranteed opening. Engagements are project-based, and livingHR will reach out when an opportunity aligns with your experience, availability, and location.



POSITION DETAILS

Location: Remote

Type: Contract / 1099

Work Model: [Remote / Hybrid]

Compensation: $50-$75/hr

Duration: TBD



WHO WE ARE

livingHR is The Work Agency™, a national boutique HR advisory and consulting firm built to make work better. We partner with organizations to deliver human-centric, future-of-work-ready HR managed services, professional services, and products. We believe in taking exceptional care of our people so they can deliver exceptional outcomes for clients, partners, and communities.



POSITION OVERVIEW

We are seeking an experienced Compensation Analyst II to support client engagements on a 1099 contract basis. In this role, you will lead market pricing and benchmarking activities, contribute to compensation program design and analysis, and deliver credible, data-driven recommendations. This is a project-based engagement requiring a professional who can work independently, manage competing priorities, and communicate compensation insights clearly to HR and business stakeholders.



RESPONSIBILITIES

• Conduct job evaluations and market pricing analyses using major compensation survey platforms

• Benchmark roles across functions and levels against external market data to inform pay decisions

• Develop and maintain salary structures, pay ranges, and job hierarchies

• Analyze internal pay equity and identify trends, anomalies, or areas requiring attention

• Support compensation review cycles including merit, bonus, and off-cycle adjustments

• Prepare compensation recommendations and present findings to HR Business Partners and leadership

• Respond to ad hoc compensation requests and provide guidance on offer recommendations

• Maintain documentation of job profiles, comp guidelines, and benchmarking methodologies

• Partner with livingHR Principals and client stakeholders to drive measurable outcomes

• Track billable work and maintain accurate project documentation



REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

• Proficiency in at least one major compensation survey platform (Radford/Aon, Mercer, WTW, or similar) is required

• 3–5+ years of compensation experience with demonstrated expertise in market pricing and benchmarking

• Strong Excel skills — comfortable with VLOOKUPs, pivot tables, and data modeling

• Solid understanding of job architecture, pay structures, and FLSA classification

• Ability to operate independently in a fast-paced, project-based consulting environment

• Strong advisory, stakeholder management, and communication skills

• Experience working in consulting, professional services, or multi-client environments



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

• CCP (Certified Compensation Professional) or other relevant HR certification

• Experience supporting pay equity analyses and total rewards program design

• Familiarity with HRIS platforms (Workday, UKG, Paylocity, or similar)

• Experience with PE-backed, high-growth, or multi-state organizations

• Willingness to travel occasionally as client needs require



WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT

livingHR engages contract consultants on a project-by-project basis. Engagement timing, scope, and duration vary based on client needs. Consultants may be considered for multiple opportunities over time as part of our long-term talent community. This engagement is structured as a contract role with the potential for extension based on client need and mutual fit.



livingHR · The Work Agency™ · livinghr.com http://livinghr.com

source_jobs.title: Electrical Engineer II
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview:

Join a family owned & operated company with 42+ years of stability & growth with industry leading products that are 100% manufactured in the USA!

With headquarters in Cleveland OH and regional facilities in Houston TX, Atlanta GA, and Reno NV, our client is North America’s largest manufacturer of flexible metal hose and metal bellows expansion joints, with over 450,000 square feet of manufacturing space.

Founded in 1982, our client has established itself as the first choice in metal hose and metal expansion joints for industrial markets including power generation, refining and petrochemical, steel, pulp and paper, chemical processing, marine and offshore, liquid terminals, and bulk material handling.

Our client is passionate about providing customer-driven designs, manufacturing excellence, and superior service. Innovation and application expertise are the cornerstones of their identity and the fuel for their growth.



POSITION SUMMARY

The Electrical Engineer II will design, wire, program, test, and troubleshoot custom equipment used in the manufacturing of company products.

 

PRIMARY JOB RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, document, and implement complex machine control systems

 - Troubleshoot and repair complex machine control systems

 - Verify safe reliable machine operation and initiate corrective action

 - Evaluate and source cost effective replacement parts

 - Create and edit programs for PLCs, HMIs, and Motion Controllers

 - Estimate cost and completion date for assigned project(s)

 - Layout and wire complex panels, controls, and machine control systems

 - Analyze and recommend improvements to equipment

 - Complete assigned projects independently

 - Handle confidential information on a regular basis

 - Provide technical guidance to Electrical Engineer I and Electrical Technician I

 - Perform other duties as directed by the Machine Engineering Supervisor and/or the Lead Electrical Engineer



QUALIFICATIONS

 - High school diploma with a minimum of 15 years; or BSEE or BSEET degree with 10 years of experience

 - Familiar with NEC, UL508A, and Industrial control application best practices

 - Must have all basic tools

 - Industrial control and power system design and troubleshooting

 - Read, create, and evaluate electrical schematics

 - Good advanced math skills

 - Familiar with programming PLCs, HMIs, drives, and motion controls

 - Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines

 - Ability to work flexible hours and overtime as required

 - Ability to use a computer and software as required

 - Project management and technical leadership

 

COMPETENCIES

 - Accountability

 - Analytical/Problem solving/Decision Making

 - Communication Skills

 - Computer Skills

 - Interpersonal Skills

 - Quality

 - Safety

 - Team Effectiveness

 - Time Management

 - Value/trust/Integrity

 

SKILLS

 - Electrical safe work practices

 - Proficient with electrical measurement tools.

 - Good communication and decision making abilities.

 - Extensive analytical and problem solving abilities.

 - Able to self-manage and self-motivate effectively.

 - Possess adaptability and flexibility traits.

 - Strong knowledge of all relevant industry practices and standards.

 - Strong administrative computer skills (e.g. Microsoft Office).

 - Proficient with CAD, PLC and HMI programming tools.

 - Effective internal and external relationship management abilities.

 - Strong technical and interpersonal communication.

 - Strong business ethics and values.

 - Strong planning and organizational abilities.

 - Aggressively maintains integrity and trustworthiness.

 - Maintain a customer focus in all duties and directives.

 

OTHER REQUIREMENTS

 - Walk up to a ¼ mile at a time.

 - Bend, squat and/or reach as needed.

 - Ability to use hand and power tools.

 - Must understand and speak English.

 - Sitting, standing, walking, lifting and/or carrying objects up to 50 lbs.

source_jobs.title: Quality Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview:

Join a family owned & operated company with 42+ years of stability & growth with industry leading products that are 100% manufactured in the USA!

With headquarters in Cleveland OH and regional facilities in Houston TX, Atlanta GA, and Reno NV, our client is North America’s largest manufacturer of flexible metal hose and metal bellows expansion joints, with over 450,000 square feet of manufacturing space.

Founded in 1982, our client has established itself as the first choice in metal hose and metal expansion joints for industrial markets including power generation, refining and petrochemical, steel, pulp and paper, chemical processing, marine and offshore, liquid terminals, and bulk material handling.

Our client is passionate about providing customer-driven designs, manufacturing excellence, and superior service. Innovation and application expertise are the cornerstones of their identity and the fuel for their growth.



POSITION SUMMARY

The Quality Engineer will play a key role in ensuring the quality of our products and maintaining high standards in our manufacturing processes. This position will be responsible for developing, implementing, and maintaining quality plans, quality control, quality control systems and standards to meet customer and regulatory requirements. The Quality Engineer will work closely with production, engineering, and other departments to drive continuous improvement in product quality and manufacturing.

 

PRIMARY JOB RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop, implement, and maintain quality control processes and procedures to ensure products meet customer and regulatory requirements.

 - Investigate and analyze non-conformities and implement corrective actions to resolve quality issues.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including production & engineering, to ensure quality standards are integrated into product development and manufacturing processes.

 - Monitor and analyze quality data to identify trends and recommend improvements.

 - Conduct internal audits to ensure compliance with company, ISO, and customer QMS requirements.

 - Provide training and guidance to production staff on quality procedures and best practices.

 - Assist with complex dimensional inspection and train inspectors on measurement techniques.

 - Participate in customer audits and respond to customer inquiries and complaints regarding quality issues.

 - Support continuous improvement initiatives by identifying opportunities for process optimization and cost reduction.

 - Keep up to date with industry trends and best practices in quality engineering.

 - Conduct inspections and tests of products, materials, and processes to identify areas for improvements and potential quality issues.

 - See assigned CARs through to completion & close out.

 - Facilitate formal root cause analysis for Corrective Actions and train others on RCA techniques as needed. Manage APQP (PFDs, PFMEAs, Control Plans, Capability Studies, MSAs) for assigned area(s).

 - Train others on inspection, measurement, and APQP methods.

 - Prepare customer QA submittals, such as ITPs, FAIRs, PPAP documents, and Quality Plans.

 

QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor's degree in engineering, quality management, or a related field.

 - 3-5 years' experience in quality engineering, preferably in manufacturing environment.

 - Strong knowledge of quality management systems and standards, such as ISO 9001.

 - Experience with quality control tools and methodologies, such as SPC, FMEA, and root cause analysis.

 - Proficient in using statistical software and quality measurement tools.

 - Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.

 - Strong communication and interpersonal skills.

 - Ability to work effectively in a cross-functional team environment.

 - Working knowledge of ASME Welding Code preferred.

 - Working knowledge of NDE (PT, VT) preferred.

 - Professional certification such as Certified Welding Inspector (CWI), is a plus.

source_jobs.title: Key Account OEM Sales Manager
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview:

Join a family owned & operated company with 42+ years of stability & growth with industry leading products that are 100% manufactured in the USA!

With headquarters in Cleveland OH and regional facilities in Houston TX, Atlanta GA, and Reno NV, our client is North America’s largest manufacturer of flexible metal hose and metal bellows expansion joints, with over 450,000 square feet of manufacturing space.

Founded in 1982, our client has established itself as the first choice in metal hose and metal expansion joints for industrial markets including power generation, refining and petrochemical, steel, pulp and paper, chemical processing, marine and offshore, liquid terminals, and bulk material handling.

Our client is passionate about providing customer-driven designs, manufacturing excellence, and superior service. Innovation and application expertise are the cornerstones of their identity and the fuel for their growth.



POSITION SUMMARY

The Key Accounts & OEM Sales Manager drives our client's growth, retention, and strategic alignment with Key Accounts as well as OEMs. This role integrates commercial, technical, and operational leadership to strengthen customer relationships, ensure performance excellence, and expand market presence in engineered flexible metal tubing solutions.

 

RESPONSIBILITIES

The Key Accounts & OEM Sales Manager will have key account ownership of a defined customer list such as Swagelok, Parker, McMaster-Carr, and Grainger with following responsibilities:

 - Grow sales through product expansion, engineered solutions and market extension by partnering with the customer leadership team, marketing/sales, sourcing, engineering, and other key departments of the customer that impact sales development.

 - Serve as the primary liaison between the customer and internal departments.

 - Work with commercial management team to develop and implement the company’s key accounts and OEM strategy.

 - Coordinate company activities around pricing, contract renewals, performance metrics as well as quality and delivery issues to the mutual satisfaction of our client and key accounts.

 - Interface with customers to conduct regular reviews of business performance.

 - Monitor market and competitive trends to position company value effectively.

 - Develop the forecast/budget for key accounts and OEMs.

 - Work with Marketing to prospect potential OEM and contractor accounts, engaging in lead generation activity to solicit new business.

 

QUALIFICATIONS & BACKGROUND

 - Bachelor’s degree preferably in engineering, business, or industrial technology (MBA preferred).

 - Credible technically and commercially so that one earns and maintains trust from engineers, procurement personnel, and other key contacts.

 - Resilient, adaptable, and self-regulating whereby one manages long-cycle, complex industrial sales processes.

 - 10- years in industrial sales or key account management. (HQ level preferred)

 - Demonstrated skills in achieving sales and budget goals.

 - Ability to effectively implement sales and marketing strategies.

 - Strong analytical skills and critical thinking skills.

 - Strong presentation and communication skills, both verbal and written.

 - Ability to interface and demonstrate leadership both internally and externally.

 - High-energy initiative-taker as well as collaborator.

 - Proven ability to effectively communicate cross functionally within the company and the customer.

 - Demonstrated commitment to building strong business relationships with customers. Ability to interpret technical drawings, specifications, and process requirements. Strong organizational skills where capturing and maintaining valuable information as well as proactively thinking through next actions and closing those actions.

 

COMPETENCIES

· Analysis and Problem Solving

· Communication

· Decision Making

· Planning and Organizing

· Safety

· Self-Management

· Values, Trust, and Integrity

· Ana

source_jobs.title: Director, Development (Non-Profit)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ORGANIZATION:


JOIN A LEADING NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION REMOVING BARRIERS FOR INDIVIDUALS, FAMILIES, AND SENIORS ACROSS THE TAMPA BAY AREA. EACH YEAR, THEY PROVIDE SUPPORT TO MILLIONS OF INDIVIDUALS/FAMILIES THROUGH A COMBINATION OF DIRECT PROGRAMS AND AN EXTENSIVE NETWORK OF COMMUNITY PARTNERS. BY CREATING PATHWAYS TO LONG‑TERM STABILITY FOR NEARLY ONE MILLION RESIDENTS, THEY BRING TOGETHER RESOURCES, PARTNERSHIPS, AND SOLUTIONS THAT FOSTER LASTING IMPACT.

 


POSITION SUMMARY


THIS IS A CRITICAL LEADERSHIP ROLE RESPONSIBLE FOR DEVELOPING AND EXECUTING FUNDRAISING STRATEGIES FOCUSED ON ANNUAL GIVING PROGRAMS, INCLUDING DIRECT RESPONSE CAMPAIGNS (MAIL AND DIGITAL) AND MONTHLY DONOR PROGRAMMING. THE DIRECTOR WILL DRIVE THE ENGAGEMENT, RETENTION, AND GROWTH OF CORE DONORS WHILE IDENTIFYING AND CULTIVATING MID-LEVEL ANNUAL DONORS. THIS INDIVIDUAL WILL COLLABORATE CLOSELY WITH DEVELOPMENT LEADERSHIP TO STRENGTHEN ANNUAL GIVING, ELEVATE DONOR STEWARDSHIP, AND BUILD A THRIVING FUNDRAISING PIPELINE ALIGNED WITH THE ORGANIZATION'S MISSION.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and nurture relationships with existing and prospective donors, cultivating a culture of giving and inspiring long-term, sustainable support.

 - Lead the development and execution of multi-channel direct response campaigns (digital, mail, phone) to engage, retain, and grow core and mid-level annual donors.

 - Manage and expand the organization's monthly giving program to foster deeper engagement, recognition, and donor advocacy.

 - Collaborate with the stewardship team to implement personalized donor engagement strategies that ensure donors feel valued and informed about their impact.

 - Identify and cultivate prospective mid and major donors within the existing donor pool, creating clear pathways for relationship upgrades and long-term support.

 - Analyze donor data to track behaviors, measure campaign performance, and refine strategies for acquisition, retention, and growth.

 - Partner with development leadership to design and execute annual fundraising strategies aligned with the organization's mission and broader revenue objectives.

 - Provide regular progress updates to the Chief Development Officer, tracking and analyzing efforts to adjust strategies as needed.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with communications, marketing, and events teams to ensure a cohesive and consistent donor experience.

 - Set and monitor fundraising goals, ensuring consistent progress toward annual revenue targets and donor base growth.

 - Identify meaningful opportunities for donor engagement, including program involvement, events, and recognition activities.

 - Maintain data hygiene protocols to ensure the integrity and accuracy of the donor database.

 - Perform other duties as assigned to support the overall success of the development team.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor's degree and 5+ years of experience in fundraising, donor relations, and annual giving, ideally within a nonprofit environment.

 - Proven proficiency with CRM systems (Salesforce preferred) and Microsoft Office and Google Suite.

 - Demonstrated ability to analyze donor data and translate insights into actionable strategy.

 - Exceptional written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills with a track record of building and maintaining strong relationships.

 - Strong project management skills with the ability to prioritize effectively and maintain attention to detail across multiple initiatives.

 - Self-motivated and proactive, with a continuous improvement mindset and collaborative team orientation.

 - Ability to exercise good judgment and discretion when handling confidential information.

 - Flexibility to work evenings or weekends as needed to meet project and organizational needs.

notes: Mostly non-tech roles, with some engineering and HR positions. Remote work options available.
